methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series E → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.4x) to $3.8B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $400M - $250.0B.

data.narrative:
Ayar Labs is a Series E artificial intelligence and software company founded by Mark Wade, currently valued at $3.75 billion post-money following $870 million in total capital raised. The valuation triangulation draws from a peer universe of nine comparable companies—one public (Palantir) and eight private firms operating in the same sector and stage, including xAI, Crusoe Energy, Groq, SandboxAQ, and WEKA.

Two quantitative methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using a 1.4x multiplier typical of Series E advancement to the next funding round, implies a valuation of $5.2 billion from the current $3.8 billion post-money base. Peer-median analysis across six same-sector, same-stage comparables yields $6.3 billion, with the peer cohort ranging from $400 million to $250 billion in valuation, reflecting the heterogeneity in late-stage AI company valuations.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $4.5 billion (low) to $7.2 billion (high), with a median estimate of $5.8 billion. This suggests upside from the current $3.75 billion valuation of approximately 55 percent to the median and 92 percent to the high case. The primary uncertainty lies in the dispersion within the peer set—the $250 billion xAI valuation and $1.6 billion WEKA valuation span three orders of magnitude—making comparable-company methodology less precise absent clarity on Ayar Labs' relative product maturity, revenue stage, and competitive positioning within frontier AI.
